meta.title: Borderline Personality–Related Traits Reflection
meta.description: A private 500-statement, non-diagnostic reflection across 20 related areas.

levels.label: Fewer Reported Difficulties
levels.desc: You reported fewer difficulties in this non-diagnostic reflection. This cannot confirm or rule out BPD or another condition.

levels.label: Some Recurring Difficulties
levels.desc: You reported some recurring emotional or interpersonal difficulties. Skills practice or a conversation with a qualified professional may help.

levels.label: Several Areas of Strain
levels.desc: Several patterns appear difficult. Consider discussing their duration, context, and impact with a licensed mental health professional.

levels.label: High Reported Pattern Load
levels.desc: Your responses suggest substantial emotional or interpersonal strain. Please consider seeking a comprehensive professional assessment and support.

levels.label: Significant Reported Distress
levels.desc: You reported significant distress or impairment. Seek professional support promptly; use emergency or crisis help now if safety is uncertain.

questions.text: Over recent months, I could experience strong feelings without becoming completely overwhelmed.
Across different settings, My emotions became so intense that they felt unbearable.
During close relationships, Intense emotions eventually reduced without controlling all of my actions.
After a disagreement, Strong feelings rapidly took over my thinking and behaviour.
When I feared rejection, I could experience strong feelings without becoming completely overwhelmed.

During ordinary responsibilities, My emotions became so intense that they felt unbearable.

After feeling excluded, Strong feelings rapidly took over my thinking and behaviour.

When another person disappointed me, My emotions became so intense that they felt unbearable.
When my emotions shifted quickly, Intense emotions eventually reduced without controlling all of my actions.
While trying to calm down, Strong feelings rapidly took over my thinking and behaviour.
Even when others could not see my distress, I could experience strong feelings without becoming completely overwhelmed.
